TextENGNew: I had only sent for Tomitake.
But Irie had come along with him.
...Irie must have been deeply troubled by what I told him this morning, unable to reach a conclusion on his own, and so he wanted Tomitake to hear my concerns as well.
After promising not to interrupt, he sat down a short distance from us...
``I heard from Director Irie...@k@vS13/08/DS41090009. Why do you suspect Takano-san?@k@vS13/08/DS41090010. She knows better than anyone what a catastrophe your death would cause...``

``...Besides, Takano-san has always been so kind to you and Satoko-chan, hasn't she...?@k@vS13/08/DS41090012. To say something like this about her now... don't you feel that's a bit unfair to her...?``
This reaction from Tomitake was entirely predictable.
Had this been this morning, I probably couldn't have continued from here.@k But since I'd perfectly anticipated this response, I was able to press on without faltering.
``...Tomitake.@k@vS13/05/DS41050196. Please answer honestly.@k@vS13/05/DS41050197. And please, think about this seriously.``
``...Under normal circumstances, I don't believe Takano would ever kill me...... but can you absolutely guarantee that the current Takano wouldn't?``
changeReason: New translation specifies 'the current Takano' (今の鷹野), emphasizing her present mental state, whereas original uses generic 'she would never do so.'

TextENGNew: ``...Wh-What do you mean by that...?``
``Takano has devoted her entire life to researching Hinamizawa Syndrome.@k@vS13/05/DS41050200. That's a fact, isn't it?``

``...Y-Yeah.@k@vS13/08/DS41090015. ...This stays between us, but she's really the heart of the research on Hinamizawa Syndrome.@k@vS13/08/DS41090016. Director Irie is the official head, but the true driving force behind the research is Takano-san.``
changeReason: Original says Takano is 'the most important person at this institution'; new says she is 'the heart of the research on Hinamizawa Syndrome' -- different characterizations of her role.

TextENGNew: ``......This is supposed to be a secret, but... the truth is, Hinamizawa Syndrome was first discovered by her grandfather.``
changeReason: Original omits dialogue quotation marks making it read as narration; new correctly frames it as Tomitake's quoted speech with added detail from the JPN.

TextENGNew: ``...Takano-san deeply reveres that grandfather.@k@vS13/08/DS41090019. She established the Irie Institute with the burning ambition to carry on and complete his research.``
changeReason: Original says Takano 'respects him greatly'; new says she 'deeply reveres that grandfather' with 'burning ambition' -- significantly stronger characterization matching JPN 意気込んで.

TextENGNew: ``...So for someone like Takano, how does it feel to have the research policy changed and be told it will end in three years?@k@vS13/05/DS41050202. ...She even went to Tokyo alone to plead for its continuation, but was turned down, wasn't she?``
changeReason: Original says 'the project will be shutting down' and she 'requested an extension'; new captures additional JPN detail: the research policy change, the three-year deadline, and her going alone to plead.

TextENGNew: ``...For Takano, that's tantamount to having her entire life denied.@k@vS13/05/DS41050204. She must be deeply wounded...``
changeReason: Original asks a question ('Couldn't you say...?'); new states it directly ('For Takano, that's tantamount to having her entire life denied') -- changes Rika's rhetorical framing from questioning to asserting.

TextENGNew: ``......Rika-chan, where did you learn all this...?``
Tomitake fell silent.
He must have realized that what I was saying was far from idle speculation.
``......It... it is true that Takano-san went to present her case to the new board of directors, and received a very unfavorable evaluation.``

``...From what I've heard, they thoroughly ridiculed her work.@k@vS13/08/DS41090023. They called it a wasteful drain on the budget.@k@vS13/08/DS41090024. ......She puts on a brave face, but... I think she was deeply hurt.``
``See?@k@vS13/05/DS41050206. So can you really say with certainty that she wouldn't grow desperate, thinking her research is going to be terminated regardless?``
``N-...No, ...not Takano-san. She would never.@k@vS13/08/DS41090026. ...It's impossible.``
The same response as Irie.
Repeating 'impossible' over and over, yet unable to deny it completely.
The very repetition was an attempt to banish a terrifying possibility he couldn't quite dismiss.
......In truth, even knowing that Takano was the enemy,@k the reason why she would plot my death remained beyond anything we could do more than guess at.
Short of tying Takano down and making her confess over a plate of katsudon, there was no way to know for certain.
changeReason: New includes the culturally significant detail about 'a plate of katsudon' (カツ丼) referencing Japanese interrogation customs, omitted in original.

TextENGNew: But there was value in making Tomitake suspicious of Takano.
...Getting Tomitake to doubt Takano and drawing him to my side was what mattered.
changeReason: Original vaguely says 'so that he'll become my ally'; new explicitly states both strategic goals: 'doubt Takano and drawing him to my side,' matching JPN dual strategy.

TextENGNew: This too, of course, was our club president's strategy.
``......It is a frightening thing to imagine, certainly... but even so, Takano-san has nothing to gain from your death.``
``I can understand the impulse toward desperation, but she has her self-control.@k@vS13/08/DS41090029. ...Time will heal those wounds, given the chance...``
``...Isn't it true that the heartbroken are especially vulnerable to manipulation?``
``Manipulation...? What do you mean by that?``
``Tomitake.@k@vS13/05/DS41050209. If I die, something catastrophic will happen, right?@k@vS13/05/DS41050210. The emergency manual will be executed, and the entire village will be annihilated.``
``Wh-- What?!@k@vS13/08/DS41090032. ......Ah, ...ahahaha, i-is that so?!@k@vS13/08/DS41090033. I-- I had no idea about that...``
...What a transparently poor attempt at deception.
It was painfully obvious. He simply had no guile, no capacity for concealment.
......Which was precisely what made him a good person.
``Irie already told me, so I know.@k@vS13/05/DS41050212. We don't have time to waste on pretense, so let's drop the act.``
``...The emergency manual is strictly a measure of last resort, so if it's actually executed, the fallout will be enormous, won't it?``

Tomitake hesitated briefly over whether to acknowledge the emergency manual's existence,@k but considering that Irie had already spoken about it and that I was myself one of the directly involved parties as the queen carrier, he grudgingly confirmed it.
``...Yes, it would be catastrophic.@k@vS13/08/DS41090035. Erasing an entire village is no simple matter.``
``Countless dangerous lines would have to be crossed, and necessary or not, it's not something that can be morally justified.@k@vS13/08/DS41090037. The consequences would be catastrophic.``
``I'll be blunt.@k@vS13/05/DS41050215. Someone must stand to gain from that emergency manual being activated.``
changeReason: Original says 'Someone will benefit from that situation'; new specifies 'from that emergency manual being activated' -- more precise about the mechanism of benefit.

TextENGNew: ``Impossible!@k@vS13/08/DS41090039. Nobody could benefit from that...!@k@vS13/08/DS41090040. If anything, it would spiral into a hunt for blame, and in the worst case, the fallout could extend beyond 'Tokyo' into the government itself...!``
``...In which case,@k@vS13/05/DS41050217. wouldn't there be someone who stands to profit from that very turmoil?``
``If the emergency manual is executed, it will be carried out as a necessary evil,@k@vS13/05/DS41050219. but afterward, the inquest into who allowed things to deteriorate that far will begin.``
changeReason: Original says 'someone forced to sign off on it'; new says 'carried out as a necessary evil' -- different readings of JPN 執行はやむなく行なう.

TextENGNew: ``...Specifically, the higher-ups overseeing the Irie Institute.@k@vS13/05/DS41050221. If those people are held responsible and toppled from their positions within 'Tokyo,' who fills the void?``
changeReason: New adds the question 'who fills the void?' about succession, introducing a dimension not in the original translation.
Original has articulate 'I'm not sure'; new has inarticulate sounds with different tag references -- different portrayal of Tomitake's reaction.

TextENGNew: ``Positions are finite.@k@vS13/05/DS41050223. There are always people vying for them.@k@vS13/05/DS41050224. Would it really be so strange if someone decided to light the fuse on this dangerous bomb called the emergency manual, just to seize a vacant seat?``
``They hide in safety, let everything blow up, then casually claim the empty chair.``
Everything I'd just said was borrowed wholesale from Mion.
changeReason: Original says 'All this is what Mion suggested'; new says 'borrowed wholesale from Mion' -- JPN 受け売り implies direct copying, not suggestion.

TextENGNew: She knew nothing whatsoever about 'Tokyo,' and yet she'd struck so close to the heart of the matter that even Tomitake, an insider, was visibly shaken...
...Mion truly was the finest commander and strategist our club could boast.
At some point, Tomitake had crossed his arms and begun to fidget restlessly.
...He seemed to be realizing that my words were far from idle rambling.

``......It's true that recently, a powerful figure in 'Tokyo' passed away.@k@vS13/08/DS41090045. His death created a power vacuum, and the factional infighting over succession has grown extremely fierce.``
``The Irie Institute's client, the Alphabet board of directors, also underwent such a sweeping personnel overhaul that it's been called a purge...``
changeReason: Original calls it 'the Alphabet Project'; new says 'the Alphabet board of directors' -- different organizational referent.

TextENGNew: ``As a result, the Irie Institute, which had been under the old faction's patronage, was singled out, and there's been talk of immediately terminating the research...``

``...In the midst of all that, if I were to die and the emergency manual had to be executed,@k@vS13/05/DS41050227. the Irie Institute, its backing board of directors, and the faction that supports them would inevitably face a reckoning.``
``......Normally, no one outside 'Tokyo' knows about the dangerous bomb that is Hinamizawa Syndrome, so it's safe.``
``...But the moment an enemy appears within 'Tokyo' itself, Hinamizawa becomes a bomb that's all too easy to detonate.``
``...............Is that... something you made up, Rika-chan...?``
``...Is it fabrication? Or is it the truth?``
``...............I-I don't know.@k@vS13/08/DS41090050. But if I think about what's going on in Tokyo... I guess... it is possible for some people to... think... things like that...``
``Tomitake.@k@vS13/05/DS41050232. You are a good person.@k@vS13/05/DS41050233. You're a very good person who believes in his close friend.

...So I know if I say these things to that Tomitake, you'll never accept them.@k@vS13/05/DS41050235. ......But I have to bring this up with First Lieutenant Tomitake, auditor for the Irie Institute.``
``............You aren't saying...@k@vS13/08/DS41090052. Are you saying that it's Takano-san?``

``Yes.@k@vS13/05/DS41050237. Her request was denied, as was her own life. Not only that, she was denounced by the board of the directors, so she's very hurt.@k@vS13/05/DS41050238. You should know that, since you're always with her, Tomitake.``

``...Is there any chance that someone seeking to use that heartbroken Takano approached her and instigated her into action?@k@vS13/05/DS41050240. People who are hurt often open their hearts to whoever claims to understand them.
Someone like that might have approached Takano to start something that will lead to chaos in Tokyo.``
Tomitake insisted that Takano-san would never do that.@k He repeated it a few more times, but then he fell silent again.
Tomitake is an adult.
He must have experienced some heartbreaking events himself.
...That's why he can understand that Takano is very hurt@k and that it's possible that someone is using her...
``...I understand what you want to say.@k@vS13/08/DS41090055. I should clear some issues first before I can truly trust Takano-san, huh?``
``Tomitake...``

``I'll get in touch with both Okonogi-kun from the Mountain Dogs and the Tokyo office to see if there have been some suspicious reports regarding Takano-san lately.@k@vS13/08/DS41090057. The Mountain Dogs' specialty is counterintelligence, after all.``
``No.@k@vS13/05/DS41050244. The Mountain Dogs are on Takano's side.``
``This is someone who persuaded Takano.@k@vS13/05/DS41050246. Of course they've taken measures for the Mountain Dogs as well.``

``......That... isn't impossible...@k@vS13/08/DS41090060. The Mountain Dogs are a special counterintelligence unit.@k@vS13/08/DS41090061. Even if there was a collaborator in the Irie Institute, it would be very difficult so long as they were attending to their duties.
......But if the Mountain Dogs themselves were... Can that be...?@k@vS13/08/DS41090063.  ...Hmmm... but......``
``Tomitake.@k@vS13/05/DS41050248. Please look into both Takano and the Mountain Dogs.@k@vS13/05/DS41050249. Irie is on my side, but he doesn't know too much about Tokyo.``

``...That's true.@k@vS13/10/DS41100068. I'm just a researcher.@k@vS13/10/DS41100069. I know my position is just a formality for their convenience.@k@vS13/10/DS41100070. That's why I can't clear Rika-san's suspicions by myself.``
``I understand.@k@vS13/08/DS41090065. I've been told that there's a possibility the Irie Institute could become rebellious.
That's the reason why I'm here.@k@vS13/08/DS41090067. I'll start investigating Takano-san and the Mountain Dogs right away.``
``.........Thank you for believing me.@k@vS13/05/DS41050251. This is the first time you actually have...``
I've been laughed away in many worlds.
But Tomitake finally listened to me.
...He will be a very strong ally in the fight against Takano.
Also, I'm grateful for my friends who prepared me to talk to him.
I got information on the current situation from Irie, had my friends analyze it, and because of that, managed to get Tomitake to listen.
Just as Hanyuu said.
I couldn't have done anything if I'd kept thinking about it all alone.
But after talking to my friends, things are already changing.
``Actually, I'm going into hiding starting today.@k@vS13/05/DS41050253. Mion's house has a secret basement that's perfect for that.``
I haven't talked to Mion about it yet...
but I think she'll understand my request now.
``So if you find anything, please get in touch with her.@k@vS13/05/DS41050255. Starting today, please act like I've gone missing.``

``...Understood.@k@vS13/08/DS41090069. I think that's safest too.@k@vS13/08/DS41090070. I'll act like I usually do.@k@vS13/08/DS41090071. So people don't grow suspicious.@k@vS13/08/DS41090072. Though I'll be careful, of course.@k@vS13/08/DS41090073. ...Dr. Irie, please act normal as well.@k@vS13/08/DS41090074. But always be on your guard.``
``I... I understand.@k@vS13/10/DS41100072. I'll be very cautious.``
``......Tomitake, please be careful yourself.@k@vS13/05/DS41050257. I have a feeling you might be assassinated within a few days.``
This is the part that he has never taken seriously.
...Please... hear what I'm saying!
``...Okay.@k@vS13/08/DS41090077. I'll be extra careful.@k@vS13/08/DS41090078. Thank you.``
Then Tomitake turned around all of a sudden.
Irie and I sensed something odd from his behavior and grew tense.
Then... we heard some footsteps.
We've been so careless. Did somebody overhear us?
Did Takano and her people hear what we were saying?
I will never forget that laugh as long as I live.

``...Hello, hello!@k@vS13/11/DS41110104. What an interesting group.@k@vS13/11/DS41110105. Dr. Irie, Tomitake-san and Rika-san.@k@vS13/11/DS41110106. I've been to Hinamizawa quite often, but I've never seen you three together before.``
``...Oh...... hello!@k@vS13/10/DS41100074. Ooishi-san.@k@vS13/10/DS41100075. Huh?@k@vS13/10/DS41100076. Is today the meeting day for Watanagashi?@k@vS13/10/DS41100077. It's still so early.``
Irie tried to change the subject, but obviously Ooishi had heard everything.
Tomitake from Tokyo is okay, but it's too early for Ooishi from the police department get involved.
Things are already complicated. Why does he have to show up now?@k Anyway... this isn't good...!
``Well...@k@vS13/11/DS41110108. when I went to your house, Houjou-san told me you were here.@k@vS13/11/DS41110109. And I happened to run into your secret meeting.
...Sorry to bother you, though.@k@vS13/11/DS41110111. ...Nfufufu!``
The air froze, leaving only silence.
The way Ooishi is speaking makes it obvious that he's trying to sniff things out.
I bet he only heard a part of our conversation, so he can't grasp exactly what we were talking about.
...That's why he's trying to make us confess the whole thing.

``Come now. Don't look at me like that.@k@vS13/11/DS41110113. I'm getting embarrassed.@k@vS13/11/DS41110114. ...Well...@k@vS13/11/DS41110115. I brought someone with me today.@k@vS13/11/DS41110116. Someone Rika-san would love to see again.
Nfufufu!@k@vS13/11/DS41110118. I wonder if you remember, Rika-san.@k@vS13/11/DS41110119. Because if you don't, he might even start crying.@k@vS13/11/DS41110120. Nfufufu!``
...What?
I can only think of one person.
But did he ever come here in June 1983?
But who else would come visit me?
``Come on in!``
Ooishi encourages a virile young man to enter. He looked a lot more mature than the last time I saw him.
``Thank you for remembering me, Rika-chan.@k@vS13/13/DS41120135. Hahahaha, you won't tell me to go back to Tokyo this time, will you?``
``...A... Akasaka... Akasakaaaaaa...!!!``
``I remember... how you helped me.@k@vS13/13/DS41120137. ...Or rather, maybe I forgot at some point.@k@vS13/13/DS41120138. But... if I forgot about it completely, I would regret it.

...So I recalled it.@k@vS13/13/DS41120140. You told me to call Yukie, and because I did, she avoided getting in an accident.@k@vS13/13/DS41120141. You saved her life.@k@vS13/13/DS41120142. And I'm here now out of gratitude.
I feel like I've forgotten about it for a very long time.@k@vS13/13/DS41120144. But now I finally remember.@k@vS13/13/DS41120145. Sorry to make you wait for so long.``

``...Th-That's right, you made me wait forever!@k@vS13/05/DS41050261. And... now you show up in this final world! ......You waited until the very last minute, how dare you!@k@vS13/05/DS41050262. Akasaka... Akasaka......!``
``I'm your ally no matter what.@k@vS13/13/DS41120147. I'll believe everything you tell me.@k@vS13/13/DS41120148. And I will defeat whoever is trying to kill you!!``
Akasaka remembered some strange things as he talked to me.

He remembered the sad world where he lost his wife working on the kidnapping case in Hinamizawa.@k He forgot about me because of the despair, and only remembered her afterwards.@k He regretted not being able to do anything for me.
That memory from a different world brought Akasaka here.
Akasaka calls himself my ally. That means a lot to me, since I never thought I'd have any allies at all.
...I could only bury my face in Akasaka's chest and cry.

``...I haven't seen anything.@k@vS13/11/DS41110123. I don't know anything about you having such a young wife here in Hinamizawa when your wife's back in Tokyo!@k@vS13/11/DS41110124. Nope, nope, don't know a thing!``
``O-Ooishi-san, no, this isn't what you think!``
``Akasaka... Akasaka...... Akasaka......... Akasaka............ Oooooooh!``
I complained that I couldn't defeat Takano alone.
Hanyuu admonished me.
She told me that if we put our strength together and all believed in the same thing, we could make a miracle happen.
I didn't believe her.
But after I talked to Irie, my friends, and Tomitake, things were getting better and better.@k And now Akasaka is here too!
Was Akasaka supposed to be coming back?
Or is this a miracle?
Miracles do occur.
This man never helped me even once before, yet he might be my most dependable ally. Now he's back, and he's even telling me he's my ally no matter what.
``.........See?@k@vS13/12/DS41070185. It happens.@k@vS13/12/DS41070186. Miracles really do occur.``

``...You're right.@k@vS13/05/DS41050265. We can make them happen.@k@vS13/05/DS41050266. If we keep putting our strength together, do you think even more miracles will happen?@k@vS13/05/DS41050267. Then...@k@vS13/05/DS41050268. do you think we can make a miracle happen, and defeat the curse of 1983?``
``Yes.@k@vS13/12/DS41070188. Because we can smash through June of 1983 only by putting everyone's strength together.``
...Hanyuu, who used to be just a bystander, is now persuading me.
She's admonishing me for pretending to fight while I, too, was actually just a sulky bystander.
``...Are you okay, Rika-chan?``
``......Y-Yes. ...Sorry about that.@k@vS13/05/DS41050270. ...Seeing you... brought back so many memories that I lost my cool.@k@vS13/05/DS41050271. ...Meep.``
I wiped my tears and tried to hide my embarrassment.
The higurashi continued to cry.
The higurashi have been trying to remind me of something very important.
...I felt like they had taught me something important, and my eyes grew teary again.
``Ha, hahaha.@k@vS13/08/DS41090080. I'm surprised!@k@vS13/08/DS41090081. I didn't know you had such a wonderful boyfriend!@k@vS13/08/DS41090082. ...Hahahaha.``
``Let me introduce myself.@k@vS13/13/DS41120152. I'm Mamoru Akasaka.@k@vS13/13/DS41120153. I work in Case Records Room 7 of the Tokyo Metropolitan Police Department's Public Security Bureau.``

``...Aren't the case record rooms in Tokyo's Public Safety Bureau a secret department on par with the CIRO?@k@vS13/08/DS41090084. ...I believe it's a tough, secret investigation unit that some have criticized as being secret police.``
No department would be foolish enough to slap ``secret`` onto the name for the secret division.
Real secret divisions have boring names meant to fool the average person.
That's true for Akasaka's department too.
It's called a reference room, but they don't manage documents.

``You seem to know quite a lot about us.@k@vS13/13/DS41120155. I didn't think there'd be someone who knows the true identity of the Public Safety department.@k@vS13/13/DS41120156. ...Judging from the conversation you were having, I take it you have your own reasons for that.``
``...Well, ha ha ha ha!@k@vS13/08/DS41090086. I'm into stuff like that.@k@vS13/08/DS41090087. I just happen to know, that's all.@k@vS13/08/DS41090088. ...Ha ha ha...``
``Tomitake-san, why don't you reveal your identity, hmm?@k@vS13/11/DS41110126. I, for one, always figured you weren't just some traveling photographer.``
``N-No! That is the truth.@k@vS13/08/DS41090090. I just like taking pictures, that's all.``
What a waste of time this conversation is.
This is the final world, after all.
``...Tomitake is the one who is connecting us and Tokyo.@k@vS13/05/DS41050273. But he's my ally just the same.``
``R-Rika-chan...!``@r@t``R-Rika-san...!``
Tomitake and Irie yelled simultaneously.
To them, it's an extremely important secret and if someone were to find out, they'd get eliminated by the Mountain Dogs.
But it's okay now.
There's no point of continuing to have suspicions and doubts.
I've had enough of that!
It's already obvious Tomitake isn't telling the truth, but he's still trying to deny what I just said.
``You're Director Irie, right?@k@vS13/13/DS41120158. Thank you for taking care of me when I was injured five years ago.@k@vS13/13/DS41120159. You really did a great job treating my wounds.``
``Ah... ahh, now I remember...@k@vS13/10/DS41100079. No, don't mention it, I only did my job.``
``The Irie Clinic is also known as the Irie Institute.@k@vS13/13/DS41120161. Right?``
``Ah... well... I mean...``
``Nfufufu!@k@vS13/11/DS41110128. Come now, Dr. Irie, it's no use.@k@vS13/11/DS41110129. We already heard it all earlier.``
``You were saying that the Irie Institute's client is the Alphabet Project, right?@k@vS13/13/DS41120163. I don't need to hear it from you, I already know everything.
Up until just recently, Room 7 was investigating the men behind it and their means for funneling vast sums of money.@k@vS13/13/DS41120165. The Alphabet Project was one of their tunnels.``
Akasaka is holding the top secret document he got his hands on a few days ago.
This document records how certain people were given public funds from ministries and government offices for assisting with various projects.
...These masterminds claimed that they were attempting to revitalize Japan by using secret money and secret methods, but that was a lie even amongst themselves.
The masterminds of these projects were making an enormous profit off of them.
If that became public knowledge, Japan would be shaken to the core, and the government would be overthrown.
That was what they were told, and that was the reason why the investigation was discontinued.

``First of all, I want to tell you one thing. The investigation by Room 7 has been shut down, and we are no longer working on the case.@k@vS13/13/DS41120167. So I'm simply telling you what I found out in the course of the investigation.
I'm on vacation here in Hinamizawa right now.@k@vS13/13/DS41120169. I just want you to know that.``
Tomitake is still trying to hide his identity,@k but Irie isn't.
He must have made up his mind, since Akasaka already knows about the Alphabet Project.
``We already know that they've been using tunnels to move vast sums of money to a research institute in Shishibone known as the Irie Institute.

Also the 'Irie Institute' has a different name here in parentheses, the 'Irie Clinic'.@k@vS13/13/DS41120172. It also lists that the head of the Irie Institute is a lieutenant colonel from the SDF,@k@vS13/13/DS41120173. Kyousuke Irie.``
``...A lieutenant colonel!@k@vS13/11/DS41110131. D-Dr. Irie, you're in... the SDF?!``
Tomitake-san, let me tell them.@k@vS13/10/DS41100085. Putting our clients aside, we aren't doing anything shady.
Let's not hide anything. It'll only make Ooishi-san more suspicious.``
``......Irie is right.@k@vS13/05/DS41050275. Ooishi will help us.``
I'm starting to feel like even Ooishi could be our ally.
That would be incredible...!
Tomitake finally gave in and admitted everything.
In his place, I explained to them the existence of Hinamizawa Syndrome, the Irie Clinic that was established to research it...
...What's special about myself...@k and finally about the invisible conspiracy that revolves around it all.
Ooishi was very surprised to hear about all this, but Akasaka told him to calm down, so he listened quietly.
Since Akasaka belongs to a special unit, he wasn't too surprised.
He kept nodding, saying that it's all very possible.
...This is working out great for me.

``Just as you say, Rika-chan, there's a good chance there are some people who are planning something like that.@k@vS13/13/DS41120175. Ooishi-san, what they are saying is well within the realm of the possible.``
``...Hahh...@k@vS13/11/DS41110133. ...This feels like the plot of a novel...@k@vS13/11/DS41110134. ...Ah...... but if it's true...... hmmm.``

Compared to Akasaka, who has a flexible mind, Ooishi is having a hard time understanding such unrealistic stories. He'll probably need at least three cigarettes before he can digest what he just heard.
``...I think Takano is suspicious.@k@vS13/05/DS41050277. But there's no proof.@k@vS13/05/DS41050278. I was asking Tomitake to investigate so that I can confirm my suspicions.``
``Ooishi-san,@k@vS13/13/DS41120177. can you arrest and detain Takano on a separate charge?``
``......Hmmm.@k@vS13/11/DS41110136. I may lose my retirement money for that, but it could be done, I guess...``
``There are some counterintelligence members from the Mountain Dogs at the police station.@k@vS13/10/DS41100088. Even if you try, Ooishi-san, there will be pressure from within the department.``
``I'll conduct an investigation on Takano-san, so if possible, can you leave it to me? I don't want anyone to get suspicious.``
``...You're right.@k@vS13/13/DS41120179. If Takano's investigation can only be done by Tomitake-san's institution, then the police shouldn't bother him.

...Besides, I have a feeling that this thing is a lot bigger than we think.@k@vS13/13/DS41120181. ...Ooishi-san.@k@vS13/13/DS41120182. I don't mean to sound rude, but maybe you should stay out of this.``
``...Akasaka-san, what do you mean by that?``
``Just like you told me earlier, you're retiring this year, right?@k@vS13/13/DS41120184. You've worked hard for many years. You're about to collect your pension.

Money is important.@k@vS13/13/DS41120186. You'll need it to pay off your house and you need money for life after retirement.@k@vS13/13/DS41120187. But this hill's too dangerous to climb.@k@vS13/13/DS41120188. My instincts are telling me that at the end of it, this case will be classified, as if it didn't take place at all.@k@vS13/13/DS41120189. In other words...``
``Unless I'm prepared to lose everything, I better pull myself out of this, right?@k@vS13/11/DS41110139. That's what you're telling me, isn't it...?``
``......Yes.@k@vS13/13/DS41120191. I know I'm not in a position to tell you that.
...But I've been involved in cases that are much different from regular police work.@k@vS13/13/DS41120193. So... what I'm saying now comes from experience.``
Ooishi was no fool.
...He was also an adult, and smart in a real sense.
If he only listened to his pride, he would yell at Akasaka for insulting him.
But what Akasaka is saying is true. Ooishi's pension is a big deal for him.
People live far beyond their retirement.
They still have ten or even twenty years left after that.
Their pension is the basis for that second life.
A pension isn't ordinary money.
It's very,@k very valuable.
People receive their pension for working hard on rainy days and windy days and even enduring the moments when their bosses give them a hard time.
Even Ooishi has mumbled, on occasion,@k that he's going to quit and look for a new job.
Yet, he endured, and quietly did the unreasonable work asked of him.
He's endured far more work than his salary merited.
He's worked like that for a very long time, and now he's finally about to retire!
He's finally about to collect his long-awaited pension.
It's not about the amount of money itself.
It's about having a value placed on what you've done with your life.
So if one were to cause a scandal, the punishment for that would be placed on his pension.
...One such deplorable event can erase Ooishi's entire life as a policeman.
That's not something that can be treated lightly.
Akasaka is still young.
Even if he loses his job, he will be able to find a new one.
But Ooishi is too old.
The repayment of his mortgage was planned around his pension.
...Ooishi is a grownup's grownup, and that's why he can't do anything reckless.
Akasaka knows that, and that's why he said that Ooishi should back down.
Ooishi understands it too.
...So... Akasaka had to ask him that tough question... in order to find out how strong Ooishi's commitment was.

``...I've been awfully slow paying back my loan, all because I wanted to hold on to some spending money...@k@vS13/11/DS41110142. Even though I would've saved on interest if I'd just paid a bit more earlier on.
...But I just wanted to gamble and drink...
If I'd lived my life so I didn't have to depend on my pension in my old age... ...I could jump right onto this case...``
Ooishi's fists were trembling.
...That's how he sheds his tears.
``But... But...!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110146. I'm an officer until the moment I retire!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110147. No, even that doesn't matter.
I promised in front of his grave.@k@vS13/11/DS41110149. I promised that old man that I'd find out who killed him!
I promised to stay a police officer until I arrest his murderer!@k@vS13/11/DS41110151. In other words... me being a police officer isn't the issue here!!
What matters is that I keep my officer's spirit until I find that murderer, even if I'm fired!
But I... I... am I afraid to be fired?@k@vS13/11/DS41110154. Am I afraid to lose my pension?@k@vS13/11/DS41110155. ...Yes, I am!!!

It'll ruin my plans for my future!! I need a lot of money for them!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110157. My dream is to live a leisurely life in Hokkaido, work on a vegetable garden, have fun in Susukino, and take up ballroom dancing lessons!
But why am I afraid?!@k@vS13/11/DS41110159. Is my officer's spirit... so weak as all that?!

I've dreamt of uncovering the truth behind the series of mysterious deaths in Hinamizawa!@k@vS13/11/DS41110161. And the door to that truth I've sought for so long is right here in front of me...!@k@vS13/11/DS41110162. Dammit! Goddammit!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110163. Why can't I just throw away my badge?!
Why can't I stop caring about what the police think?!!@k@vS13/11/DS41110165. Dammit, dammit, goddammit!!``
...Nobody could say anything in response to Ooishi.
...He's at a crossroads. The crossroads of a person who has worked so hard and so humbly for so long.
How could the young people who know nothing about what he's had to go through say anything at all?
``...You are... a friend of the departed construction site manager, right?``
``............Yes.@k@vS13/11/DS41110167. He was a friend, a brother, and a father to me.``

``Although it's only a formality, I am the director of the Irie Institute.@k@vS13/10/DS41100091. If the conspiracy that surrounds Rika-chan is exposed, I will be the one who takes the blame.@k@vS13/10/DS41100092. ...Isn't that right, Tomitake-san?``
His position exists for exactly that purpose.
...To the Insitute as a whole, Irie is there simply to cover for Takano's research.
...Tomitake, of course, knows about that, and even Irie has started to realize it.
Even if Irie hadn't done anything wrong, he would have to take the blame, as the person in charge of the Institute.
...That was how every organization purged itself: by forcing someone to take the blame and reshuffling members.
That's why leaders get more money.

``...When it's all over, I will tell you everything... about the series of mysterious deaths.@k@vS13/10/DS41100094. ......I actually have an obligation to remain silent for the rest of my life, but you'll let me tell him, won't you, Tomitake-san?``
``...Hahahaha.@k@|@y@vS13/08/DS41090095. ......I didn't hear anything.``
As an honest man, Tomitake can't say yes.
So pretending to ignore it was the best he could do.
``...No, no... that's not right.@k@vS13/11/DS41110169. ...You can tell the brave Kuraudo Ooishi about it, the one who found the courage to help you.
The cowardly Kuraudo Ooishi, the one who didn't want to lose his pension, doesn't deserve to hear the truth.``
He faced a dilemma. Even though the truth behind the mysterious deaths he sought so hard was right in front of him... ...he couldn't just accept it with his head held high.
To understand what he's going through, one would have to live the life he did.
...His dilemma is very vast and very heavy.
...Young people couldn't possibly understand.

``...Can I... have some time to think about this?@k@vS13/11/DS41110172. I really do.... want to find the truth, and put evil behind bars.@k@vS13/11/DS41110173. ...I just need some time to gather my courage. ...Could you grant me that...?``
Why is he even asking?
Nobody here will force him to make up his mind.
...Of course, I won't either.
However... Ooishi needs someone's approval.
Ooishi is still passionate about his work as a police officer, yet he isn't brave enough to take on this case. He wants somebody to forgive him for that.
...But nobody can do that, and nobody can stop Ooishi from crying...
``...Yes.@k@vS13/12/DS41070190. I will grant you that time.``
Hanyuu?
How long has she been here?
Hanyuu has arrived. I didn't see her come in.
...But then again, she's always around.
Hanyuu is the guardian deity of Hinamizawa, after all.
``...I'll grant you some time to make your decision.@k@vS13/12/DS41070192. ...You have been honest, and you've been tough on yourself until today.
Your life is not to be taken lightly, and that's why you are having such a hard time deciding.@k@vS13/12/DS41070194. One should never be proud of careless decisions or brute courage.
...A careful judgment made by a person who has lived a worthy life, like you... that's what's worth being proud of.``
She might sound arrogant to us... but that was exactly what Ooishi needed to hear.
Right... who among us has the right to admonish Ooishi, the eldest one here?
No one. No one should be able to.
...Nobody but one. ...The one who has been protecting humans for over a thousand years. ...Hanyuu.
``...No matter if you decide calmly or courageously,@k@vS13/12/DS41070197. I will praise you.@k@vS13/12/DS41070198. As long as your decision was made well.``
``......I've never met you before, but thank you.@k@vS13/11/DS41110175. ...That makes me feel better.@k@vS13/11/DS41110176. ...Thank you for giving a coward like me the time of day.``
If it was me, I would have asked Ooishi to be my ally, and asked him for help.
But that wouldn't have reached his heart.
Wanting help is my selfish request.
I don't know anything about the life he lived, and that's why my words wouldn't reach him.
But Hanyuu's words did.
Hanyuu has never told me this, but I can tell she's been watching Ooishi since the day he was born.@k She's been watching all that he went through.
That's why she can say that.
And why what she said could reach him.
Only Hanyuu can do that.
``......Dr. Irie.@k@vS13/11/DS41110178. ...There's only one thing.@k@vS13/11/DS41110179. Please tell me one thing.``
``...Sure.@k@vS13/10/DS41100096. Ask me anything.``
``......I've long suspected that the Sonozaki family was pulling the strings in the old man's murder case.@k@vS13/11/DS41110181. ...Am I right to think that...?``
``.....No, you are not. You're wrong.@k@vS13/10/DS41100098. We can explain everything about the series of mysterious deaths in Hinamizawa.``
Ooishi pretended to scratch his head.
...For so long he pursued the case with that belief... yet someone deeply involved was telling him he'd missed the mark... so he must be feeling at least some frustration.
What Irie said wouldn't be enough.
So I have to continue.

``......Ooishi.@k@vS13/05/DS41050280. None of the curses of Oyashiro-sama were done in secret by the villagers.@k@vS13/05/DS41050281. The Sonozaki family, the Three Families, and the other villagers... none of them are involved.``
There were only misunderstandings, misinterpretations, coincidences... tragedies, and comedies.
The real identity of the curse of Oyashiro-sama is simply a series of misinterpretations. It only looks like the villagers are acting in secret.
``...So, all the information I've gathered so far about the Sonozaki family@k@vS13/11/DS41110184. is worthless?@k@vS13/11/DS41110185. ...Hahaha.``
``...The heads of the Sonozaki family have a secret precept. They must always act as if they command even natural disasters.@k@vS13/05/DS41050283. ...The Sonozaki family knows nothing.
Moreover, they are even afraid of the incidents that occur every year.@k@vS13/05/DS41050285. But in order to maintain their dignity, they act as though they're the ones behind everything.``
``Oh my goodness...@k@vS13/10/DS41100100. ...I see. ......It all makes sense now.``

``............Hahaha, ha ha ha ha ha!@k@vS13/11/DS41110187. If that's true... then that Oryou woman... damn it... she sure said some things to mislead me!@k@vS13/11/DS41110188. Hahahahahaha, ha ha ha ha ha ha!
You're telling me the truth, aren't you?``
``...Yes, I am.@k@vS13/05/DS41050287. It's written in the Furude family's secret scrolls too.@k@vS13/05/DS41050288. Do you want to see?``
``...Hahahahaha, ha ha ha ha ha ha!!!``
I have no idea what Ooishi thinks is so funny...
Nobody here can tell what's going through Ooishi's mind.
......Except the girl who has watched over human beings for over a thousand years.
``...I can understand why you're so happy.``
``What? ...What do you mean...?``
``The thought that you don't have to hate anyone anymore is a very, very welcome concept.``
``Ha ha ha, oh no.@k@vS13/11/DS41110192. The Sonozaki family still is a vile organization with its claws all over Shishibone City. That doesn't change.
...I've been stabbed by one of them before, you know?@k@vS13/11/DS41110194. They may not be responsible for the mysterious deaths, but they are still my enemies.
...That's all still true, but I just feel happy.@k@vS13/11/DS41110196. ...Nahahaha.``
None of us can tell if what Hanyuu is saying is right.
Ooishi believed the Sonozaki family was behind it all.
Now that he's learned that they're not, I'm sure he feels like everything he stands for has been made meaningless.
But if Hanyuu says that's not the case...
To hate someone must be such a painful thing.
I can't explain Ooishi's laughter. All I can do is speculate.
Ooishi left with a cigarette in his mouth, saying that he wanted to cool off a little.
``...We won't get anywhere until we know if Takano-san is guilty or not.@k@vS13/10/DS41100102. So we won't be able to act until after we hear the report from Tomitake-san.``
``I don't know why Rika-chan is so suspicious of Takano-san... Personally, I feel bad for her...@k@vS13/08/DS41090097. But what she said makes sense too.
...Rika-chan is an important person.@k@vS13/08/DS41090099. And we can't let anyone suspicious get near her.``
Tomitake is making it sound like he's going to prove Takano's innocence.
I guess I can't blame him, since he's in love with her.
...To be honest, I'm also hoping that Takano isn't guilty.
Takano understands me, and she was one of my allies.
...I had a friendship with her. I've trusted her for over a hundred years.
But Hanyuu says
that Takano is guilty.
...If something pops out when we poke her, ...then the question is how well Tomitake can catch it...
To get people to help me, I have to find proof of Takano's guilt.
So I just have to trust Tomitake for now.
``Tomitake-san.@k@vS13/13/DS41120195. Please be very careful.@k@vS13/13/DS41120196. If she is guilty, then you'll be in danger too.``

``I understand.@k@vS13/08/DS41090101. I'll move to a new hotel without letting the Institute know.@k@vS13/08/DS41090102. I should probably leave Hinamizawa immediately, but if Takano-san is guilty, that would backfire.``
``That's pretty tough, yeah.@k@vS13/13/DS41120198. It's a cardinal rule to act normally during a secret investigation.@k@vS13/13/DS41120199. Would you prefer to stay with me?``
``No, that's alright.@k@vS13/08/DS41090104. It's fine, I'll be very careful.``
``...But Rika-chan's safety is the priority here...@k@vS13/13/DS41120201. What should we do?``
``......What should we do?@k@vS13/05/DS41050290. Hanyuu, what should we do?``
``Using shogi as an example, you're like an unprotected king.@k@vS13/12/DS41070203. As soon as Takano gets ahold of you, the game is over.``
``One of the most important rules in shogi is to guard the king first,@k@vS13/05/DS41050292. right?``
``Yes.@k@vS13/12/DS41070205. Today is Thursday.@k@vS13/12/DS41070206. Watanagashi is in three days.@k@vS13/12/DS41070207. You need to hide somewhere nobody can find you until at least then.``
``Any idea where?``
``...I think you should ask Mion.``
``That's true.@k@vS13/05/DS41050295. The Sonozaki family's underground storehouse would be an ideal place to hide.``
``Satoko should go with you too.``
``If I leave Satoko behind, they'll question her about my whereabouts.@k@vS13/05/DS41050297. She may be in danger too.``
...That means I'll have to tell the truth to at least Satoko and Mion.
...That the comic plot we discussed wasn't just fiction.
...Still, I always seem to underestimate their reliability. It really is beyond my imagination.
I need to talk to them.
I have a feeling they'll believe me.
They're my steadfast allies, after all.
``...We're going into hiding for a while.@k@vS13/05/DS41050299. There's a perfect place for that at Mion's house.``
``Will you really be safe there?``
``...Yes. It's like an air-raid shelter.@k@vS13/05/DS41050301. It has everything we need. We can easily stay there for about a month.``
The Sonozaki family's underground storehouse has every basic necessity, in case someone actually had to live there due to some complication or another.
...Mion has mentioned before that it's like a unique vacation house.
...I don't know how you can call a place with a torture room a vacation house, though.
``I don't know how long my investigation into Takano-san will take,@k@vS13/08/DS41090106. but as soon as I find out something, I'll call Mion-chan's house. Is that okay?``
``...That works fine.``
``To tell you the truth... The Mountain Dogs have a bug on Rika-chan's home phone for security reasons.@k@vS13/08/DS41090108. ...So that works better either way.``
``......Meep.@k@vS13/05/DS41050304. So somebody has been listening to all of my personal conversations?``

``N-No, it's set up so they can tune in, but that doesn't mean they're listening to every normal conversation.@k@vS13/08/DS41090110. They only do it when they have a security drill. That happens a few times a year.``
``...I bet my phone at the clinic is being wiretapped too, huh?``
``...Well, yes.@k@vS13/08/DS41090112. That's because of the purpose of the facility, though... please understand.``
``Excuse me.@k@vS13/13/DS41120204. How do they actually do that?``
``Rika-chan's land line branches off.@k@vS13/08/DS41090114. Do you remember the little shack behind the shrine?@k@vS13/08/DS41090115. The line leads into there, which is where they wiretap it.

Usually there's no one there, but once the Mountain Dogs receive orders to guard her, there will be two agents recording conversations in there all the time.@k@vS13/08/DS41090117. Of course, they don't usually record anything.``
``Is there a security drill scheduled in the near future?@k@vS13/13/DS41120206. Or maybe orders to guard her?``
``Security drills take place in January, April, July, and October.@k@vS13/10/DS41100105. So there's no drill this month.
Orders to guard her are given only when they anticipate an emergency, but they haven't been given even once since the Institute was established.``
``Who gives those orders?``
``The director does, which is me. But Takano-san is a surrogate director, so she can send out orders too.``
``...If Takano really is the person who wants to kill Rika-chan, then she'll use the Mountain Dogs to spy on her.``
``That's very possible.``

``In other words,@k@vS13/13/DS41120210. if people start to go in and out of that shack, it means that Takano has made her move.@k@vS13/13/DS41120211. So we could take that as proof that tensions are building around Rika-chan, right?``
``...I don't know if that would prove that Takano-san is behind it,@k@vS13/10/DS41100110. but we can take it as a sign that danger is approaching.``

``Indeed.@k@vS13/13/DS41120213. Regardless of whether Takano is guilty or not, that's what it means.@k@vS13/13/DS41120214. As long as we keep our eyes on the shack, Rika-chan will stay safe and secure.@k@vS13/13/DS41120215. That's a great idea.``
``...But there is one problem, Rika.@k@vS13/12/DS41070211. If there are no lights on in the house at night, it'll be obvious to everyone that there's nobody home.
Takano doesn't even have to wiretap your phone. As soon as she sees the house, she'll realize you're skipping school and ran away.``
``You're right.@k@vS13/05/DS41050306. And that puts Tomitake, who's investigating Takano, in a tight spot.@k@vS13/05/DS41050307. We'll have to think of something to counteract that.``
``So we're dealing with having to observe the wiretapping shack, like Akasaka was saying earlier.@k@vS13/12/DS41070214. Then there's the problem with having to keep the lights on at your house.

It's also a fact that it's better for Akasaka to stay in the village. If I put all three together and mix them up, I get...@k@|@y@vS13/12/DS41070216. au au au.@k@|@y@vS13/12/DS41070217. I have an idea.``
``...Oh no.@k@vS13/05/DS41050309. You aren't thinking about letting Akasaka stay at my house, are you?@k@vS13/05/DS41050310. No, no, no, no, no, no, absolutely not!!!
My house is such a mess, it smells and it's filthy, I can't let Akasaka stay in there. NO, NO, NO!!!``
``Au au au au, Akasaka is staying oveeer! Au au au au☆.``
``I said no!!@k@vS13/05/DS41050313. No, no, no, no, no, noooooo!!!``
``Why are you making such a fuss, Rika-chan?``
``...M-Meep~.@k@vS13/05/DS41050315. I lost my cool there for a moment.@k@vS13/05/DS41050316. Nipah~☆...``
G-Geez! Just because Hanyuu's insisting... Okay?
Au au au au!!
``It's true that if Rika-chan's house ends up empty, there won't be any lights on at night.@k@vS13/13/DS41120217. And leaving them on would be even more suspicious.``
``Ah... ah, Akasaka.@k@vS13/05/DS41050318. ......Ah... ...Meep.@k@vS13/05/DS41050319. What do you think of staying at my house while I'm gone?``
``I see...@k@vS13/13/DS41120219. That's a great idea.@k@vS13/13/DS41120220. As long as it's okay with you, Rika-chan, let's do that.``
Akasaka seems to think it's a pretty good idea.
...Am I selfish for wanting him to get a little embarrassed about staying at my house?
...I should be ashamed of myself... Au au au.
Everything is set.
Tomitake is going to get in touch with his colleagues in Tokyo and start an investigation on Takano.
...That's all.
That's all we can do from our end.
Irie is going to lead his normal life.
To act normally after something like this will be pretty difficult, but there really isn't anything else for him to do right now.
Takano is sly. She'll never let Irie ruin her plans.
Actually, Irie only comes into the picture after Takano's been exposed.
Ooishi didn't say he would help, but he said he wouldn't interfere with us.
In fact, there are some people with ties to the Mountain Dogs at the police department too.
It's probably best if Ooishi acts normally, like Irie is.
I'm going to tell Satoko the truth and hide at Mion's house for a while, starting today.
I don't know how long I'll have to hide, but it'll be at least until something happens.
Akasaka is going to stay at my house and make it look like I'm home, sick.
If something happens, coming all the way from Okinomiya would take too long.
It feels much better to have him stay in the village.
Everything will begin only once Tomitake finds out something.
Irie says he hopes things will get moving soon... but I don't think we need to worry about that.
They will try to kill Tomitake on the night of Watanagashi, on Sunday, two days from today.
That has never changed in many, many worlds, so that's definitely their plan.
In other words, even if Tomitake doesn't find anything, the wheels will start turning in two days.
I had warned Tomitake again to be extremely careful.
...But still!
If I still get killed, I'll curse your family for seven generations after I die.
It's already getting dark.
Everyone went home except for Akasaka.@k I'm still thinking about how to tell Satoko about Akasaka, and also about what's to come.
``Worrying about it won't solve anything.@k@vS13/12/DS41070220. Rika, you have a bad habit of underestimating your friends.``
``But...@k@vS13/05/DS41050321. how can she believe that what we talked about at school today is actually real?
How can she believe me when I tell her that her life is in danger?@k@vS13/05/DS41050323. In addition, I'm bringing home this guy she's never met before!``
``Don't you think Satoko will believe you?``
``...I don't know, but it's possible that she won't.``
``Oh,@k@vS13/12/DS41070223. so Satoko isn't your friend, is she?``
What Hanyuu just said startled me. It was kind of a scary thing to hear, even though she said it while wearing her usual warm smile.
``I know Satoko is one of us. I think she will believe you and become your ally against Takano.@k@vS13/12/DS41070225. But you're saying you can't trust her.
That's like saying Satoko isn't your friend.@k@vS13/12/DS41070227. If she isn't even your friend, then why waste your time telling her about it? Au au au.
Let's just leave Satoko behind and go to Mion's house by ourselves. Just the two of us.``
``Wh... What are you saying?@k@vS13/05/DS41050327. That's nonsense!@k@vS13/05/DS41050328. Satoko is my best friend and...``
``If you don't think it's worth telling her the truth, then that's it.@k@vS13/12/DS41070230. You just think a best friend is like a stray cat that you feed everyday.
I'm actually surprised to learn that you've been letting Satoko live with you when you think of her like that.``
``I didn't say that!@k@vS13/05/DS41050330. Satoko is my best friend!@k@vS13/05/DS41050331. My very best!!@k@vS13/05/DS41050332. Yeah, she's even closer to me than you are!!``

``But you don't believe she will believe.@k@vS13/12/DS41070233. It's the same as spouting your complaints at a stray cat that you feed every day.@k@vS13/12/DS41070234. You know the cat can't do anything, and knowing that you still complain to her, and that's all your relationship is.
Au au, poor Satoko!@k@vS13/12/DS41070236. She believes you're her best friend, but you don't feel the same way.@k@vS13/12/DS41070237. Au au au!``
``Damn you!!``
I tried to slap Hanyuu, but I couldn't touch her.
My hand went through her cheek and hit only the air.
...I can't believe she made herself incorporeal again!
Hanyuu disappeared, saying ``au au`` in a teasing manner.
...I'm upset... but I can totally understand what she was getting at.
``Will your roommate agree?``

``I'll make her agree.@k@vS13/05/DS41050335. Satoko has nothing to do with this conspiracy, but since she lives with me, she's going to end up involved anyway.@k@vS13/05/DS41050336. For Satoko's sake, I have to explain this to her.``
``...Should I wait outside?``
``No,@k@vS13/05/DS41050338. I think it'll be better to have you with us so she doesn't think it's a joke.@k@vS13/05/DS41050339. If I can't explain it properly, can you help?``
I went up to the second floor with Akasaka.
To be honest, I couldn't even imagine how to start bringing it up.
...But even Hanyuu made fun of me.
I can't hesitate.
I made up my mind and walked into the room... and was surprised.
My messy room is all cleaned up.
Satoko is packing her pajamas and towels into her duffle bag.
It's as if she's going on a camping trip.
``Here, I got your luggage ready too!@k@vS13/04/DS41040094. We can't just borrow toothbrushes and towels from Mion-san, you know.
Besides, we can't let some strange man stay in a messy room either!@k@vS13/04/DS41040096. This is why I always tell you to tidy things up around here!``
I blinked in surprise.
I don't understand why Satoko already knows what's going on.
If she saw me, Irie, and Tomitake talking behind the shrine with Ooishi and a strange man, she'd be sure to get curious.
Satoko must have overheard our conversation.
Not only that, she packed our luggage and cleaned our room, which means she already knows Akasaka is going to stay in this house.
She must have come home in a hurry to get things done.
``......So it wasn't just a storyline for some comic, was it?``
``You know, that's what I don't like about you, Rika.``
``Have I ever doubted you before?@k@vS13/04/DS41040100. I always thought that I was your best friend, that you would count on me before anyone else.``
``...I am sorry.``
Satoko doesn't seem that angry.
Rather, she seems shocked that what we talked about at school is real. She isn't really that upset.
But this is exactly what Hanyuu said.
I thought Satoko was my best friend, yet I couldn't trust her completely.
Having realized this, I apologized.
Satoko turned around as if to tell me there's no time for apologies.@k@|@y I think she accepted it.
As the proof of that, she's getting things ready to go to Mion's house.
Thank you, Satoko.
``Umm, Akasaka-san, is it?@k@vS13/04/DS41040102. I don't know how you and Rika know each other, but if you're Rika's ally, then you're mine too.@k@vS13/04/DS41040103. You may call me Satoko Houjou!``
``Nice to meet you.@k@vS13/13/DS41120226. I'm Akasaka.@k@vS13/13/DS41120227. The conspiracy surrounding Rika-chan runs pretty deep, and it's not easy to explain.
She was afraid you'd reject her after she confessed to everyone, so please try to understand how she felt too.``
``Of course I understand!@k@vS13/04/DS41040105. I'm Rika's best friend, you know.
She was thinking that I didn't believe her, who would?@k@vS13/04/DS41040107. I can understand why she'd be afraid.``
``...You're really mature, Satoko-chan.@k@vS13/13/DS41120230. Rika-chan is lucky to have you as her best friend. I'm pretty envious of her.``
Satoko took Akasaka around the house and explained everything.
She let him know that the switch for the hot water heater sticks a little and that if the picture on the TV gets messed up, he needs to tap on it.
She even told him about all the traps she set around the shrine, using a secret map of hers.
...Akasaka listened to her dead seriously.
Upon seeing them talk, I couldn't help but smile.
Hanyuu also smiled while watching us...
but it felt like she was saying ``I told you so``.
I'm going to pretend that I don't notice her.
``...If our contrarian Satoko believed me this easily, then... ...our simple-minded Mion will believe us even more easily.
Keiichi doesn't even know how to doubt people and Rena has sharp instincts, so she'll believe me too.@k@vS13/05/DS41050347. So everyone is going to believe me, huh?``
Hanyuu didn't reply, but she smiled as if she's happy to see that I grew a little.

``Alright, there're plenty of things in the refrigerator.@k@vS13/04/DS41040109. Rika!@k@vS13/04/DS41040110. You're asking him to house-sit,@k@vS13/04/DS41040111. so why don't you make dinner for him tonight?
Besides, just to be safe,@k@vS13/04/DS41040113. perhaps we should go over to Mion-san's house a little later.``
``Oh, don't worry.@k@vS13/13/DS41120232. I can handle that myself.``
``...Au au, this is a chance to serve him your homemade food, Rika. Au au au☆.``
``Y-You really are annoying today!``
``Oh right, Satoko, we have to make something good, huh?@k@vS13/05/DS41050350. I have an idea. Can you bring out the large kimchi bottle from the refrigerator?

No, not that one. The one that says 'death penalty', not the one that says 'punishment'.@k@vS13/05/DS41050352. Let's make kimchi fried rice today.@k@vS13/05/DS41050353. Akasaka, do you like spicy food?``
``Oh, yes!@k@vS13/13/DS41120234. I love spicy things!``
``Au!!@k@vS13/12/DS41070240. Au au au au au au au au!!``
``The cream puffs in the refrigerator have expired, so let's throw them away.@k@vS13/05/DS41050355. Ahh, what a waste.@k@vS13/05/DS41050356. I heard they're from a famous shop over in Gogura...``
``Au au au au au au auuu!!!!``
``...The 'au aus' are awfully loud today.@k@vS13/05/DS41050358. Maybe a stray dog's howling somewhere!``
Night is falling on the 16th of June.
The pieces are lining up on the final game board one after another.
No.
``Pieces`` isn't the right word to call them.
Night is falling on the 16th of June.
